name: Sword in the Stone
description: The Sword in the Stone is a legendary sword deeply embedded within a large stone (or sometimes an anvil on a stone) in Arthurian mythology. Its origins are often attributed to Merlin, the powerful sorcerer and advisor, who placed it there as a test to determine the rightful king of Britain. The inscription on the sword, varying slightly across different versions of the story, typically declares that whoever can draw the sword from the stone is the true-born king. The sword itself is not inherently magical in terms of offensive or defensive capabilities, but its power lies in its symbolism. It represents the divine right to rule, legitimacy, and the ability to unite the warring factions of Britain. Many tried and failed to remove the sword, demonstrating their unworthiness or lack of destiny. Arthur, initially unaware of his royal lineage, effortlessly drew the sword, proving his worthiness and fulfilling the prophecy. This act established Arthur as the rightful king, setting the stage for his reign and the establishment of the Knights of the Round Table. The Sword in the Stone is a potent symbol of destiny, worthiness, and the power of a true leader to unite a divided kingdom. It represents the transition from chaos to order, and the fulfillment of a preordained fate. It is often conflated with Excalibur, the sword Arthur later receives from the Lady of the Lake, but they are distinct objects in many versions of the myth. The Sword in the Stone represents Arthur's initial claim to the throne, while Excalibur symbolizes his power and authority as king.
subtitle: The Test of Kingship
origin: Placed by Merlin as a test of kingship
currentLocation: Mythical; presumed lost or destroyed after Arthur's reign

keyMyths.title: The Prophecy
keyMyths.description: Merlin prophesied that the true king would reveal himself by drawing the sword from the stone. Many knights and nobles attempted the feat, but all failed, highlighting the importance of destiny and worthiness.

keyMyths.title: Arthur's Discovery
keyMyths.description: Arthur, initially a squire, unknowingly drew the sword to provide one for his foster brother, Sir Kay. This act revealed his destiny and established him as the rightful king, much to the surprise of those present.

sources.author: Sir Thomas Malory
sources.description: A comprehensive compilation of Arthurian legends, featuring the Sword in the Stone as a pivotal event.
sources.text: A comprehensive compilation of Arthurian legends, featuring the Sword in the Stone as a pivotal event.

sources.author: T.H. White
sources.description: A modern retelling of the Arthurian legend, offering a unique perspective on the Sword in the Stone.
